title: GitHub Actions Execution Model, Contexts, and State Boundaries · Automation Case: Ci Context Expression Env Timing
summary: A team confuses workflow, run, job, step, action, runner, shell, workspace, environment file, output, artifact, and cache state while debugging a missing build result. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci context expression env timing increment in workflow execution and state trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish server-side contexts and expression evaluation from runner-side environment expansion, shell parsing, and process state.
Trace event, workflow run, job graph, runner allocation, step process, action execution, and post-step cleanup for one commit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Trace identity, lifetime, authority, and cleanup. content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-execution-model-contexts-5
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-execution-model-contexts-5
    artifact: execution-trace.yaml
    decision: worked gha execution model contexts 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      event: pull_request
      workflow-ref: owner/repo/.github/workflows/ci.yml@refs/pull/42/merge
      run-id: 8842
      run-attempt: 2
      job-runner: fresh-ubuntu-24.04
      step-process: isolated-shell
      state-handoff: validated-job-output
      retained-artifact: test-report-digest
    verify: compare event payload workflow ref workflow sha run id attempt runner and artifact metadata
    failure: Reject shared-runner assumptions, branch-only identity, or cache and artifact state treated as equivalent.
    repair: Trace server and runner evaluation, name each state lifetime, and retain exact run and artifact identity.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
